briefing_memo: GC Strategies Inc. received over $19 million for work on the ArriveCan app and approximately $258 million in total federal contracts since 2015. On June 6, 2025, the federal government banned GC Strategies from all government contracts for 7 years and deemed the company ineligible following assessment of supplier conduct, including allegations reported by the Globe and Mail in October 2023 of collusion with a CBSA Director General and falsification of employee credentials (source: edmonton.citynews.ca, toronto.citynews.ca, CBC). No material remediation, settlement, or integrity certification has been documented; the Auditor General reported in February 2024 that the government overpaid for ArriveCan and CBSA mishandled the file, with at least 28 contracts awarded without competition. Given the current 7-year ineligibility determination imposed less than one year ago with no evidence of remediation, recommend maintaining the contractual ban and excluding GC Strategies from any federal procurement opportunities.

actions.title: Enforce existing 7-year procurement ban across all departments
actions.rationale: GC Strategies is subject to an active government-wide contract bar (June 2025) related to ArriveCan misconduct; immediate compliance verification required.

actions.title: Verify no active contracts or payments remain outstanding
actions.rationale: Organization barred from contracts in 2025 following ArriveCan allegations; confirm all disbursements ceased and no residual agreements exist.

actions.title: Request status update on any ongoing investigations or enforcement actions
actions.rationale: Multiple CRITICAL-severity articles and CBSA misconduct allegations (2023-2025) suggest potential ongoing investigative or legal proceedings requiring monitoring.
